headline: Tech Weekly: Tesla's surge, robot footballers target glory
summary:
STORY: From Tesla driving up sales to robot footballers competing in their own competition...

This is Tech Weekly.

:: Tech Weekly

Tesla beat Wall Street estimates when it posted record second-quarter deliveries.

The electric vehicle maker delivered more than 480,000 vehicles between April and June - up about a quarter from a year ago.

The results were partly driven by a rebound in Europe.

China was another bright spot, although demand was still under pressure in the U.S.

The World Cup witnessed a technological first during Norway's 2-1 win over Brazil in the Round of 16. 

An advanced humanoid robot called Atlas delivered the match ball to the referee at half-time.

The robot was developed by U.S. firm Boston Dynamics and presented by tournament sponsor Hyundai Motor.

Atlas can impersonate famous goal celebrations, including Norway striker Erling Haaland's meditation pose.

While Atlas delivered a ball, robots in South Korea showed they could actually play soccer.

They competed at the finals of RoboCup 2026 in Incheon - a major robotics and AI competition.

Organisers said more than 3,000 competitors from 45 countries took part, and many teams reportedly used platforms developed by China's Booster Robotics.

The robots stumbled around and collided with rivals but got back up to keep playing, with Team Hephaestus of Tsinghua University winning the contest.

Google lost its long fight against a record $4 billion plus fine imposed by the EU eight years ago.

The European Commission had punished the tech giant for its dealings with phone manufacturers.

The phone makers were forced to pre-install Google Search, the Chrome browser and the Google Play app store on their Android devices - and prevented from using rival systems.

A Google spokesperson said the judgment didn't account for its efforts to make sure Android is open, interoperable and free.

And customers at the Lounge X coffee shop in Seoul are now assisted by a robot named Baris.

Its a technological sight which has become more common across South Korea.

Lounge X CEO Kim Dongjin said the firm turned to robots and self-service to overcome rising labor costs.

Kim says his company runs eight such unstaffed 24-hour coffee shops across South Korea, while four stores are still operated by humans.

headline: Why Analysts Prefer SpaceX to Tesla Stock
summary: Wall Street analysts prefer  SpaceX.  to  Tesla  among Elon Musk’s publicly traded companies.  Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, BofA, RBC, Wells Fargo, Bernstein, UBS, were among the firms launching coverage, and only one rates the stock a Hold—the rest are Buy.  The average analyst price target for Tesla stock is about $404, according to FactSet.

content.title: Why Analysts Prefer SpaceX to Tesla Stock
content.summary: Wall Street analysts prefer  SpaceX.  to  Tesla  among Elon Musk’s publicly traded companies.  Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, BofA, RBC, Wells Fargo, Bernstein, UBS, were among the firms launching coverage, and only one rates the stock a Hold—the rest are Buy.  The average analyst price target for Tesla stock is about $404, according to FactSet.

headline: This Week In Energy Transition - India's Path to Net Zero by 2050: A Renewable Revolution
summary: The global energy transition is being significantly accelerated by India's ambitious plan to become a Net Zero economy by 2050, necessitating a shift to renewable energy sources and innovative technologies. Central to this transformation is India's target to install 450 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030, alongside a transition to electric vehicles, the commercialization of hydrogen energy, and increased ethanol blending. Complementing these efforts are carbon capture and storage...

headline: Tesla stock target lifted at RBC on potential SpaceX merger
summary: Investing.com -- RBC Capital lifted its price target on Tesla to $500 from $475 in a note on Tuesday, incorporating a premium tied to a potential combination with SpaceX, while also updating its standalone intrinsic valuation of the electric vehicle maker.
